title: CI gates: schema validation, citation-coverage, no-self-approval, numeric-verification, readability, copy/similarity, no-egress
context: wash-guides enforces content quality through automated CI gates rather than manual spot-checks. These gates ensure every merged unit is schema-valid, fully cited, not self-approved, numerically verified (for any dosing/ratio/contact-time claim), readable by the target audience, free of verbatim copied source text, and — for any rendered output — accessible and not making network egress. Depends on the content schema (data-003) being finalized.
objective: Implement and wire up all mandatory CI checks: schema validation against the WashUnit schema, 100% citation coverage (every claim cited), no-self-approval enforcement (approvedBy !== reviewedBy), numeric-verification completeness (every numericClaim has verifiedBy and expertConfirmed), readability check against the plain-language target level, copy/similarity check flagging verbatim source reuse, and — for any rendered output — WCAG/a11y and no-egress (CSP connect-src 'none' + runtime network interception) checks. pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint must all pass.
acceptanceCriteria: CI runs schema validation and fails on any content unit that does not conform to the WashUnit schema
CI enforces 100% citation coverage: any claim without a named authoritative citation with retrieval date fails the build
CI enforces no-self-approval: build fails if approvedBy equals reviewedBy on any unit
CI enforces numeric-verification completeness: any numericClaim missing verifiedBy or expertConfirmed fields fails the build
CI runs a readability check and flags any unit exceeding the plain-language target reading level or step length defined in the style guide
CI runs a copy/similarity check and flags verbatim reuse of source text above a defined threshold
For any rendered output: CI runs automated a11y checks (axe/pa11y) with zero critical issues, and a runtime no-egress check that fails if any unexpected outbound network request is made
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint all pass in CI on a known-good content unit, and at least one check correctly fails on a deliberately malformed unit in the test suite
output: A CI workflow with all required content-integrity gates wired up and passing, enforcing schema, citation, no-self-approval, numeric-verification, readability, copy/similarity, and (for rendered output) a11y and no-egress checks.
